what_it_does: This terrain ledger entry describes the llama-swap model-router configuration directory at /home/tim/llama-swap: an 8K folder holding a single config.yaml that /usr/local/bin/llama-swap reads via systemd to expose an OpenAI-compatible endpoint on 127.0.0.1:9090 and to swap two small Qwen models in/out on demand through vllm-tests launch scripts with a 600-second idle TTL.
observations: Only config.yaml lives in the 8K directory; the llama-swap binary is external at /usr/local/bin/llama-swap.
The systemd ExecStart explicitly uses -config /home/tim/llama-swap/config.yaml and -listen 127.0.0.1:9090 with -watch-config.
Two model aliases, qwen2b and qwen0.8b, both invoke /home/tim/vllm-tests/serve_2b.sh with a ttl of 600.
Frontmatter lists status as unconfirmed even though the service is wired and enabled.
Relations include launched-by company-systemd, launches vllm-tests, depends-on vllm-env, and part-of company.
The service unit description tags the entry as (test).
standouts: The -config flag in ExecStart is the concrete proof that the service reads this specific directory.
It is a router, not an inference engine; actual serving happens in vllm-tests.
Idle TTL of 600 seconds implies automatic unloading to save resources.
Status unconfirmed conflicts with the evidence of active wiring.
concerns: Status is unconfirmed despite the service being enabled and the config file being read.
Coverage claims 1 of 1 files read, but the directory may contain runtime artifacts not captured.
launched_by and launched-by appear as separate frontmatter keys with different formatting.
Model alias keys (qwen2b, qwen0.8b) differ from the canonical model names in the frontmatter.
notes: This file anchors the llama-swap router config, showing exactly how the systemd unit binds to config.yaml and routes two Qwen models through vllm-tests scripts.
questions: Why is status unconfirmed if the service is enabled and the config was read?
What does /home/tim/vllm-tests/serve_2b.sh do and how does it activate vllm-env?
Which clients or tools consume the 127.0.0.1:9090 endpoint?
What version or build of /usr/local/bin/llama-swap is deployed?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the entry states it documents the llama-swap router config and service wiring, and the body does exactly that.
apparent_themes: model multiplexing behind a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint
resource conservation via idle TTL unloading
external engine configuration as a first-class ledger node
separation of router config from inference backend
intention_signals: It appears designed to provide a local, systemd-managed, on-demand model router that exposes multiple small Qwen models through one OpenAI-compatible port while keeping idle backends unloaded.
novelty: It treats a router configuration directory as a distinct terrain engine node, separate from both the binary and the vLLM backends, and documents the service-to-config wire via the -config flag as evidence.
contribution: This file contributes the authoritative mapping between the llama-swap systemd service and its configuration, plus the exact command lines and TTL policy that route models to vllm-tests; a fused system would keep this wiring evidence and relationship graph.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain ledger entry for /home/tim/llama-swap, an 8K directory containing llama-swap's config.yaml that routes two Qwen models through vllm-tests serve_2b.sh scripts behind a systemd-managed OpenAI-compatible endpoint at 127.0.0.1:9090 with a 600-second idle TTL.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to document and anchor the llama-swap model-router configuration, its systemd launch wiring, and its dependencies on vllm-tests and vllm-env.
